item.title: 构建自定义无头 CMS 的 5 个以上技巧 | 5+ Tips For Building Custom Headless CMS
item.description:
您是否正在寻找构建自定义无头 CMS （内容管理系统）但不知道从哪里开始？ 想知道无头CMS的优点是什么？ 最好使用的工具和技术是什么？ 创建自定义无头 CMS 的步骤是什么？ 它们允许更灵活地呈现内容，并可用于为用户创建个性化体验。 构建 自定义无外设 CMS 可能是一个复杂且耗时的过程，但可以使用正确的工具和知识自行完成。

在本文中，我们将讨论什么是无外设 CMS、构建自定义无外设 CMS 的好处以及如何构建无外设 CMS。 无论您是经验丰富的Web 开发人员还是新手，本指南都将为您提供开始使用自己的自定义无外设 CMS 所需的信息。

什么是无头 CMS？

使用 CMS，您可以创建一个网站，而不必从头开始编码（甚至根本不知道如何编码）。

对于传统的 CMS，内容在与表示层相同的系统中存储和管理，这意味着对表示层所做的任何更改都会影响存储的内容。 使用无头 CMS，内容与表示层分开存储和管理，允许开发人员在不影响内容的情况下对表示层进行更改。 这使得向用户呈现内容的方式具有更大的灵活性。

传统 CMS 和 Headless CMS 有什么区别？

传统 CMS 的目标是成为管理内容和前端的单一解决方案，而无头 CMS 严格处理内容，对前端没有影响或输入。 在无外设 CMS 中创建和编辑的内容通过API发布，无论内容如何呈现，都可以灵活地以尽可能多的方式获取内容。

为什么选择无头 CMS？

它的主要优点是它允许通过 API 端点将内容分发到多个渠道和设备。 这意味着内容可以推送到手机、智能手表、平板电脑、物联网设备、CRM、数字标牌等。 选择无头 CMS 以包含其功能的原因：

  • 简化即将进行的重新设计。
  • 为用户提供更好、更个性化的体验。
  • 提高前端生产性能。
  • 开发人员和设计人员应该有更多的自由和灵活性。

构建自定义无头 CMS 的好处

由于与传统 CMS 相比具有许多优势，Headless CMS 作为内容管理系统越来越受欢迎。 由于无外设 CMS 将用户体验的责任转移到客户端（例如浏览器或应用程序），因此它提供了几个好处。 这些包括增加的灵活性、可扩展性和可定制性，以及减少的开发时间和成本。 借助无头 CMS，组织可以创建可跨多个平台和设备使用的内容，从而提供更一致的用户体验。 此外，无头 CMS 更安全且更易于维护。

好处 #1 – 灵活的开发

好处 #2 – 改善体验

前端开发人员可以摆脱后端结构的限制，为他们提供更多时间和资源来创建更丰富、响应更快的数字体验。

好处 #3 – 提高速度

以不同的方式呈现给客户，可以让后端流程比以往任何时候都更快、更高效地运行。

好处 #4 – 低成本

无头 CMS 的成本通常低于传统 CMS，具体取决于客户的选择、设置的复杂性和所选的业务定价模型。

好处 #5 – 缩短上市时间

通过将前端与后端分离，可以显着减少开发产品所需的时间。 同样的原则可以应用于内容创建团队，使他们能够专注于内容本身，而不是它在不同前端层上的显示方式。 这也允许使用和重复使用一个来源。

优势 6 – 易于使用

这是因为重点是内容本身，而不是广泛的演示选项。

好处 #7 – 灵活性

开发人员可以利用无头 API 从他们所需的客户端中提取数据，具有很强的通用性。

优势 #8 – 云可扩展性

云就绪是大多数无外设系统的一个关键特性，允许它们扩展以满足长期增长，并通过使用多个环境、集群、CDN 和其他先进技术来适应流量激增。

好处 #9 – 系统安全

安全问题必须由企业解决。 此外，API 经常隐藏在几行代码后面，通常只能阅读。 最后但同样重要的是，无头 CMS 管理员通常位于与主平台不同的服务器和域上。

选择自定义无头 CMS 时要考虑什么？

在您决定采用无头模式之后，必须考虑一些重要的事情。 其中之一是这个重要的问题：在众多无头 CMS 系统中，您应该选择哪一个？ 这是一个不应掉以轻心的决定，但也没有必要不知所措。 选择任何软件时，都有很多功能和组件需要考虑，但这五个在选择无头 CMS 时必不可少。

一、业务需求

考虑您的业务对无头 CMS 的需求，并评估主要利益相关者在内容管理系统中的需求。 您的组织需要将内容发布到哪些渠道和设备？ 营销和开发团队希望从 CMS 中得到什么？ 由于无头 CMS 往往比传统系统对技术要求更高，因此开发团队对于提供所需的数字体验至关重要。 评估不同的无头 CMS 选项，以确保满足每个人的需求。

在决定使用无头 CMS 时，您必须考虑开发团队的支持以及开发人员的可用性。 开发团队将选择前端编码和用户界面框架，但评估后端使用的原生 SDK 和 API 至关重要。 成熟的REST API、安全和预览 API 以及内容管理 API 等功能对于审核至关重要。 此外，还应考虑 API 调用的最大数量以及 GraphQL 数据操作和查询的可用性等问题。 为了进一步提高开发者的易用性，需要确保系统的原生SDK兼容开发团队偏好的编码框架、语言和技术。

重要的是要了解这种跨渠道发布功能需要什么，以及哪些渠道与您的组织最相关。 网站和移动应用程序是必不可少的，但如果您需要获得不太常规的输出，例如 IoT 设备、数字标牌和语音控制的 UI，那么在后端准备好支持这些渠道的 API 就很重要。 一个好的无头 CMS 将具有内容 API，可以将标题、图像、正文和其他内容以适合设备或界面的格式推送到各种渠道。 它还应该生成确保您的内容在所有平台上可见所需的元数据。 例如，Drupal 可以生成 schema.org 标签来优化语音搜索的内容。

无论 CMS 的技术功能多么先进，内容仍应是焦点。 根据 CMS，创建和创作角色的过程可能会有所不同，因此请确保选择最适合负责填充它的作者和编辑的需求的过程。 对于拥有多个用户的大型组织，系统应该能够提供不同的访问权限，并且在考虑成本时应该考虑在内。 此外，CMS 应支持多个工作流程以确保内容得到妥善管理。 最后，检查系统在多个设备上预览内容的能力，以确保它符合要求。

就内容管理系统而言，就像任何其他软件解决方案一样，技术和销售支持必不可少。 现场开发人员可能能够处理许多问题，但有时您需要与客户联系以寻求帮助。 为避免将来出现困难，请确保您选择的 CMS 提供商拥有足够的支持人员，可以全年 24/7 访问。

自定义无头 CMS 的成本

由于 SaaS 业务及其基于请求的定价模型通常具有隐藏性，因此很难准确估计无外设 CMS 的成本。 在决定之前，您应该仔细考虑系统的预计生命周期成本，以及从长远来看是否值得投资。 确保了解所需解决方案的定价方式、影响成本的因素以及解决方案的预计生命周期成本是多少。

提防好得令人难以置信的承诺； 尽管无头 CMS 开始时可能并不昂贵，但随着流量的增加，它们可能需要额外的资源。 大多数无头 CMS 解决方案在成本方面与传统内容管理系统相当。 根据业务的不同，您可能还必须购买额外的解决方案来补充数据库，例如表示层。 另一方面，一些现代无头 CMS 已经内置了表示层，减少了对额外解决方案的需求，从而降低了总体成本。

如何构建自定义无头 CMS

创建自定义无外设 CMS 可能是一项艰巨的任务，但使用正确的工具和知识，您可以自己完成。 通过无代码开发，您可以自定义系统以满足您的业务需求，而无需成为编码专家。 随着业务的发展，由于Flatlogic平台提供的灵活性，您可以轻松地扩展和调整 CMS。 使用 Flatlogic 平台，享受传统系统的可扩展性和您需要的定制功能。

如何使用 Flatlogic 平台创建自定义 Headless CMS？

使用Flatlogic Full-Stack Generator，您可以在几分钟内创建CRUD和静态应用程序。 要开始使用该平台，您需要在 Flatlogic 网站上注册。 单击标题中的“登录”按钮将允许您注册一个 Flatlogic 帐户。

第 1 步：选择技术栈

在此步骤中，您将设置应用程序的名称并选择堆栈：前端、后端和数据库。

第 2 步。选择入门模板

在此步骤中，您将选择Web应用程序的设计。

步骤 3. 架构编辑器

在此步骤中，您可以从头开始创建数据库模式、导入现有模式或选择建议的模式之一。

要导入现有数据库，请单击“导入SQL”按钮并选择您的 .sql 文件。 之后，您的数据库将在架构编辑器中打开，您可以在其中进一步编辑数据（添加/编辑/删除实体）。

如果您不熟悉数据库设计并且难以理解什么是表，我们准备了一些现成的实际应用示例模式，您可以根据自己的应用进行修改：

  • 电子商务应用；
  • 时间跟踪应用程序；
  • 书店；
  • 聊天（消息）应用；
  • 博客。

或者，您可以定义数据库模式并通过单击“使用 AI 生成”按钮添加描述。 您需要在文本区域中键入应用程序的描述，然后点击“发送” 。 应用程序的架构将在大约 15 秒内准备就绪。 您可以立即点击部署或查看结构以进行手动调整。

接下来，您可以连接您的 GitHub 并将您的应用程序代码推送到那里。 或者单击“完成并部署”按钮跳过此步骤，几分钟后，您的应用程序就会生成。

结论

尽管这项技术存在挑战，但智能策略和下一代功能可以帮助克服这些挑战。 灵活性对于未来的成功至关重要，允许企业选择最适合其需求的组件。



原文:

Are you looking to build a custom headless CMS (Content Management System) but don’t know where to start? Wondering what are the advantages of a headless CMS? What are the best tools and technologies to use? What are the steps to creating a custom headless CMS? Web development is an activity of trust.

Headless CMSs are becoming increasingly popular and are used by many companies to power their websites and applications. They allow for greater flexibility in how content is presented and can be used to create personalized experiences for users. Building a custom headless CMS can be a complex and time-consuming process, but it is possible to do it yourself with the right tools and knowledge.

In this article, we’ll discuss what a headless CMS is, the benefits of building a custom headless CMS, and how to build one. Whether you’re a seasoned web developer or just starting, this guide will provide the information you need to get started on your own custom headless CMS.​​

What is a Headless CMS?

CMS stands for Content Management System, which is a kind of software that enables non-technical individuals to create, manage, and alter material on websites. With a CMS, you may make a website without having to start from the beginning with the coding (or even know how to code at all).

Headless CMS is a type of content management system that separates the content from the presentation layer. With a traditional CMS, the content is stored and managed in the same system as the presentation layer, meaning that any changes made to the presentation layer will affect the content that is stored. With a headless CMS, the content is stored and managed separately from the presentation layer, allowing developers to make changes to the presentation layer without affecting the content. This allows for greater flexibility in how the content is presented to users.

What is the Difference Between Traditional CMS And Headless CMS?

Traditional CMS aims to be a single solution to managing both the content and the frontend, whereas a headless CMS deals strictly with the content and has no impact or input on the frontend. Content created and edited in a headless CMS is published via an API, providing the flexibility to get the content out in as many ways as possible regardless of how it’s presented.

Why Choose Headless CMS?

Headless CMS is a content management system where the backend and frontend are fully disconnected, having no front end at all. Its main advantage is that it allows content to be distributed to multiple channels and devices through API endpoints. This means content can be pushed to phones, smartwatches, tablets, IoT devices, CRMs, digital signage, and more. A Headless CMS is an ideal solution for businesses that need to publish content to various places.

Reasons to choose a headless CMS to include its ability:

  • Simplify upcoming redesigns.
  • Provide users with a better, more individualized experience.
  • Improve front-end production performance.
  • Developers and designers should have more freedom and flexibility.

Headless CMS offers a great advantage to developers as it allows them to use the latest technologies to create a future-proof digital experience, due to its compatibility with any frontend presentation layer or device.

Benefits of Building a Custom Headless CMS

Headless CMS is gaining popularity as a content management system due to its many advantages over traditional CMS. Because a headless CMS shifts the responsibility of the user experience onto the client (such as a browser or an app), it offers several benefits. These include increased flexibility, scalability, and customizability, as well as decreased time and cost of development. With a headless CMS, organizations can create content that can be used across multiple platforms and devices, which allows for a more consistent user experience. Additionally, headless CMSs are more secure and easier to maintain.

Benefit #1 – Flexible Development

Headless technology allows developers to create apps, sites, and solutions on multiple platforms using their preferred programming languages, without being tied to any specific frontend technology.

Benefit #2 – Improved Experiences

Frontend developers can be freed from the constraints of backend structures, providing them with more time and resources to create richer, more responsive digital experiences.

Benefit #3 – Increased Speed

Presenting to the client differently makes it possible for backend processes to function more quickly and efficiently than ever before.

Benefit #4 – Low Costs

The cost of a headless CMS is usually less than that of a traditional CMS, depending on the customer’s choices, the complexity of the setup, and the chosen business pricing model.

Benefit #5 – Reduced Time to Market

By separating the frontend from the backend, the time it takes to develop a product can be significantly reduced. This same principle can be applied to the content creation team, allowing them to focus on the content itself rather than how it will appear on different frontend layers. This also allows for one source to be used and reused.

Benefit #6 – Ease-to-Use

Headless CMS is essentially a database, meaning that it may offer fewer options in terms of switches and sliders for content creators. This is because the focus is on the content itself, rather than on a wide range of presentation options.

Benefit #7 – Flexibility

Developers can take advantage of the headless API to extract data from their desired clients with great versatility.

Benefit #8 – Cloud Scalability

Cloud-readiness is a key feature of most headless systems, allowing them to scale to meet long-term growth and accommodate traffic surges through the use of multiple environments, clusters, CDNs, and other advanced technologies.

Benefit #9 – System Security

Security concerns must be resolved by the business. Moreover, the API is frequently hidden behind several lines of code and is typically just accessible for reading. Last but not least, a headless CMS administrator is often situated on a separate server and domain from the main platform.

What to Consider While Choosing a Custom Headless CMS?

After you have settled on the decision to go headless, a few important things must be taken into consideration. One of which is this important question: Out of the many headless CMS systems out there, which one should you choose? This is a decision that should not be taken lightly, but there is no need to be overwhelmed. With any software selection, there are a lot of features and components to think about, but these five are essential when selecting a headless CMS.

1. Business Needs

Consider what your business needs from a headless CMS, and take stock of what key stakeholders are looking for in a content management system. What channels and devices does your organization need to publish content to? What do the marketing and development teams hope to get out of a CMS? As headless CMSs tend to be more technically demanding than traditional systems, the development team will be essential in delivering the desired digital experience. Evaluate the different headless CMS options to ensure everyone’s needs are met.

2. Developer functionality and ease of use

When deciding on a headless CMS, you must consider your development team’s buy-in, as well as the usability of developers. The development team will select the frontend coding and user interface frameworks, but it is crucial to evaluate the native SDKs and APIs utilized on the backend. Features such as mature REST APIs, secure and preview APIs, and content management APIs are essential to review. Moreover, consideration should be given to issues like the maximum number of API calls and the availability of GraphQL data manipulation and queries. To further improve usability for developers, make sure that the system’s native SDKs are compatible with the coding frameworks, languages, and technologies preferred by the development team.

3. Omnichannel capability

Headless CMS’s omnichannel capability is one of its most important features. It’s important to understand what this cross-channel publishing capability entails, and which channels are most relevant to your organization. Websites and mobile apps are essential, but if you need to reach less conventional outputs such as IoT devices, digital signage, and voice-controlled UIs, it’s important to have APIs on the back end that are ready to support these channels. A good headless CMS will have content APIs that can push headlines, imagery, body copy, and other content to various channels in the appropriate format for the device or interface. It should also generate the metadata needed to ensure your content is visible across all platforms. For instance, Drupal can generate schema.org tags to optimize content for voice search.

4. Content and authoring role workflow

No matter how advanced the technical features of a CMS are, the content should remain the focal point. Depending on the CMS, the creation and authoring role processes can vary, so make sure to select the one that best fits the needs of the authors and editors who will be responsible for populating it. For larger organizations with multiple users, the system should be able to provide different access permissions and should be taken into account when considering the cost. Additionally, the CMS should support multiple workflows to ensure the content is properly managed. Lastly, check the system’s ability to preview content across multiple devices to make sure it meets the requirements.

5. Technical and sales support

When it comes to content management systems, just like any other software solution, technical and sales support is essential. Developers on-site may be able to handle many issues but occasionally you will need to get in touch with the client for help. To avoid future difficulties, make sure the CMS provider you choose has adequate support staff that can be accessed 24/7 throughout the year.

Cost of Custom Headless CMS

It can be difficult to accurately estimate the cost of a headless CMS, due to the often hidden nature of SaaS businesses and their request-based pricing model. Before deciding, you should carefully consider the projected lifetime cost of the system, and whether it will be worth the investment in the long run. Make sure to know how your desired solution is priced, what factors can affect the cost, and what the estimated lifetime cost of the solution is.

Beware of promises that seem too good to be true; although headless CMS can be inexpensive to start with, they can require extra resources as traffic increases. Most headless CMS solutions are on par with traditional content management systems in terms of cost. Depending on the business, you might also have to purchase additional solutions to complement the database, such as a presentation layer. On the other hand, some modern headless CMS have presentation layers already built in, reducing the need for extra solutions and thus the overall cost.

How to Build a Custom Headless CMS

Creating a custom headless CMS can be a daunting task, but with the right tools and knowledge, it is possible to do it yourself. Flatlogic Platform offers a simple solution to create a custom headless CMS system, granting you full control over your source code. With no-code development, you can customize your system to match the needs of your business without needing to be a coding pro. As your business evolves, you can scale and adjust your CMS with ease due to the flexibility that the Flatlogic Platform offers. Enjoy the scalability of a traditional system with the tailored features that you need – with Flatlogic Platform.

How to Create Custom Headless CMS with Flatlogic Platform?

Using the Flatlogic Full-Stack Generator you can create CRUD and static applications in a few minutes. To start using the Platform, you need to register on the Flatlogic website. Clicking the “Sign in” button in the header will allow you to register for a Flatlogic account.

Step 1. Choosing the Tech Stack

In this step, you’re setting the name of your application and choosing the stack: Frontend, Backend, and Database.

Step 2. Choosing the Starter Template

In this step, you’re choosing the design of the web app.

Step 3. Schema Editor

In this step, you can create your database schema from scratch, import an existing schema or select one of the suggested schemas.

To import your existing database, click the Import SQL button and select your .sql file. After that, your database will be opened in the Schema Editor where you can further edit your data (add/edit/delete entities).

If you are not familiar with database design and find it difficult to understand what tables are, we have prepared some ready-made sample schemas of real applications that you can modify for your application:

  • E-commerce app;
  • Time tracking app;
  • Book store;
  • Chat (messaging) app;
  • Blog.

Or, you can define a database schema and add a description by clicking on the “Generate with AI” button. You need to type the application’s description in the text area and hit “Send”. The application’s schema will be ready in around 15 seconds. You may either hit deploy immediately or review the structure to make manual adjustments.

Next, you can connect your GitHub and push your application code there. Or skip this step by clicking the Finish and Deploy button and in a few minutes, your application will be generated.

Conclusion

Headless CMS provides a combination of a database and an API for distributing content to any channel, making it an unbeatable choice for an ever-changing digital landscape. Although there are challenges to this technology, smart strategies, and next-generation capabilities can help to overcome them. Flexibility will be essential for future success, allowing businesses to choose the components that best suit their needs.
item.author: alesiasirotka

item.title: 计算机编程中的不同模型 | Different models in computer programming
item.description:
计算机编程中有多种模型，它们提供了设计和实现软件应用程序的结构和方法。 一些常用的模型是：

瀑布模型：

这是一个顺序模型，它遵循线性和顺序的软件开发方法。 它包括五个阶段：需求、设计、实施、测试和维护。

瀑布模型是一种线性顺序的软件开发方法，其中开发过程分为不同的阶段。 每个阶段都必须在下一个阶段开始之前完成，并且一旦完成就不能再回到上一个阶段。 瀑布模型的五个阶段是：

这涉及采访利益相关者和最终用户以了解他们的需求和期望。
这包括创建详细的系统架构、数据流图和用户界面模型。
这涉及编码、测试和调试。
这包括单元测试、集成测试和系统测试。
这包括持续支持、错误修复和更新。

敏捷模型：

这是一个迭代计算机网络模型，专注于工作软件的快速开发和交付。 它强调开发团队和利益相关者之间的灵活性和协作。

敏捷模型是一种迭代和增量的软件开发方法，强调灵活性、协作和持续改进。 与瀑布模型不同，敏捷模型不依赖于具有明确定义的阶段的严格顺序过程。 相反，它专注于以小而频繁的增量交付工作软件，同时适应不断变化的需求和反馈。

敏捷模型基于四个核心价值观：

  • 流程和工具上的个人和交互
  • 综合文档之上的工作软件
  • 客户协作优于合同谈判
  • 通过遵循计划来应对变化

原型模型：

该模型涉及创建软件的早期版本，用于收集反馈并在最终实施之前完善设计。

原型模型是一种软件开发模型，涉及创建原型并支持java 或要开发的软件的早期版本中的委托事件模型。 原型模型的目标是使用户和利益相关者能够在开发过程的早期与软件交互并提供软件反馈，以便在开发最终版本之前可以进行任何必要的更改。

原型模型通常涉及以下阶段：

这可能涉及创建用户故事、用例和其他文档以捕获系统的功能和非功能需求。
原型可能是最终系统的简化版本，或者它可能只包含最终系统中要包含的功能的子集。
反馈用于改进原型的设计并对需求进行任何必要的更改。
螺旋模型：

这是一个迭代模型，结合了瀑布模型和原型模型的元素。 它包括四个阶段：计划、风险分析、开发和测试以及评估。

螺旋计算机网络模型是一种在整个软件开发过程中强调风险管理的软件开发模型。 该模型由一系列迭代循环或螺旋组成，允许对正在开发的软件进行持续评估和改进。
螺旋模型通常涉及以下阶段：

该团队还为螺旋的每次迭代定义了可交付成果。
该团队还确定了这些风险的潜在解决方案。
反馈用于改进软件的设计。

这是瀑布模型的一种变体，它强调在开发的每个阶段进行测试和验证的重要性。 它包括四个阶段：需求、设计、实施和测试。

该模型强调在整个软件开发过程中进行测试的重要性，并且特别强调测试与需求之间的关系。

需求作为开发过程的基础，用于为模型的测试阶段创建测试用例。
设计作为开发过程的蓝图，用于为模型的测试阶段创建测试用例。
实施阶段包括编码、单元测试和集成测试。
测试阶段包括系统测试、验收测试和用户测试。

这些模型中的每一个都有其优点和缺点，模型的选择取决于项目的具体要求和约束。



原文:

There are several models in computer programming, which provide a structure and methodology for designing and implementing software applications. Some of the commonly used models are:

Waterfall model:

This is a sequential model that follows a linear and sequential approach to software development. It consists of five phases: requirements, design, implementation, testing, and maintenance.

The waterfall model is a linear sequential approach to software development, where the development process is divided into distinct phases. Each phase must be completed before the next one can begin, and there is no going back to a previous phase once it is completed. The five phases of the waterfall model are:

1. Requirements gathering: In this phase, the requirements for the software are gathered and documented. This involves interviewing stakeholders and end-users to understand their needs and expectations.
2. Design: In this phase, the software design is developed based on the requirements gathered in the previous phase. This includes creating a detailed system architecture, data flow diagrams and user interface mockups.
3. Implementation: In this phase, the software is developed based on the design created in the previous phase. This involves coding, testing, and debugging.
4. Testing: In this phase, the software is tested to ensure that it meets the requirements and functions as expected. This includes unit testing, integration testing, and system testing.
5. Maintenance: In this phase, the software is deployed and maintained in the production environment. This includes ongoing support, bug fixes, and updates.

Agile model:

This is an iterative computer network model that focuses on the rapid development and delivery of working software. It emphasizes flexibility and collaboration among the development team and stakeholders.

The Agile model is an iterative and incremental approach to software development that emphasizes flexibility, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Unlike the waterfall model, the Agile model does not rely on a rigid and sequential process with well-defined phases. Instead, it focuses on delivering working software in small, frequent increments, while adapting to changing requirements and feedback.

The Agile model is based on four core values:

  • Individuals and interactions over processes and tools
  • Working software over comprehensive documentation
  • Customer collaboration over contract negotiation
  • Responding to change by following a plan

Prototype model:

This model involves creating an early version of the software, which is used to gather feedback and refine the design before the final implementation.

The Prototype model is a software development model that involves creating a prototype and supports the delegation event model in java, or an early version, of the software to be developed. The goal of the Prototype model is to enable users and stakeholders to interact with and provide feedback on the software early in the development process so that any necessary changes can be made before the final version is developed.

The Prototype model typically involves the following phases:

1. Requirements gathering: In this phase, the development team works with the stakeholders to gather the requirements for the software to be developed. This may involve creating user stories, use cases, and other documentation to capture the functional and non-functional requirements of the system.
2. Prototype design: In this phase, the development team designs and develops a prototype of the software. The prototype may be a simplified version of the final system, or it may include only a subset of the features to be included in the final system.
3. Prototype review: In this phase, the stakeholders review the prototype and provide feedback on its functionality, usability, and other aspects. The feedback is used to refine the design of the prototype and make any necessary changes to the requirements.
4. Final product design and development: Based on the feedback received during the prototype review, the development team designs and develops the final version of the software.

Spiral model:

This is an iterative model that combines elements of the waterfall model and the prototype model. It consists of four phases: planning, risk analysis, development and testing, and evaluation.

The Spiral computer network model is a software development model that emphasizes risk management throughout the software development process. The model consists of a series of iterative cycles, or spirals, that allow for continuous evaluation and improvement of the software being developed.
The Spiral model typically involves the following phases:

1. Planning: In this phase, the development team identifies the objectives of the project, as well as the risks and constraints associated with the project. The team also defines the deliverables for each iteration of the spiral.
2. Risk analysis: In this phase, the development team identifies and analyzes the risks associated with the project. The team also identifies potential solutions to these risks.
3. Engineering: In this phase, the development team creates a prototype of the software, based on the objectives and deliverables defined in the planning phase.
4. Evaluation: In this phase, the stakeholders evaluate the prototype and provide feedback on its functionality and usability. The feedback is used to refine the design of the software.

V model:

This is a variant of the waterfall model that emphasizes the importance of testing and verification at each stage of development. It consists of four stages: requirements, design, implementation, and testing.

The V model is a software development model that is based on the Waterfall model and compatible with the delegation event model in java. The model emphasizes the importance of testing throughout the software development process, and it places a particular emphasis on the relationship between testing and requirements.

The V model consists of a series of stages, with each stage being associated with a corresponding testing stage. The stages of the V model typically include the following:

1. Requirements gathering: In this stage, the requirements for the software are gathered and documented. The requirements serve as the basis for the development process, and they are used to create the test cases for the testing stages of the model.
2. Design: In this stage, the high-level design of the software is created. The design serves as the blueprint for the development process, and it is used to create the test cases for the testing stages of the model.
3. Implementation: In this stage, the software is developed based on the requirements and design created in the previous stages. The implementation stage includes coding, unit testing, and integration testing.
4. Testing: In this stage, the software is tested to ensure that it meets the requirements and design. The testing stage includes system testing, acceptance testing, and user testing.

Each of these models has its strengths and weaknesses, and the choice of model depends on the specific requirements and constraints of the project.

它还包括运行付费社交媒体广告活动以覆盖更广泛的受众并实现特定的业务目标。

一些常见的社交媒体营销策略包括：

定期创建和分享引人入胜的内容，让关注者对品牌或产品感兴趣并了解相关信息。

Yoga Media Social Media Company

Posted on Apr 5

   

Social media marketing (SMM) refers to the practice of promoting a brand, product, or service on social media platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, YouTube, and others.

SMM involves creating and sharing content such as text, images, videos, and links to engage with the target audience, build brand awareness, and drive website traffic or sales. It also includes running paid social media advertising campaigns to reach a wider audience and achieve specific business objectives.

Some common social media marketing strategies include:

Creating and sharing engaging content regularly to keep followers interested and informed about the brand or product.

Responding to comments, questions, and messages from followers to build trust and engagement.

Running paid social media ads targeting specific demographics, interests, or behaviors to reach a wider audience and increase brand visibility.

Collaborating with influencers or other brands to expand reach and build brand partnerships.

Analyzing data and metrics to optimize social media campaigns and improve ROI.

Effective social media marketing requires a deep understanding of the target audience, knowledge of the best practices and trends in each platform, and the ability to create engaging content that resonates with followers.

item.title: Unindent 不匹配任何外部缩进级别 (Python) | Unindent does not match any outer indentation level (Python)
item.description:
如果你是 Python 开发者，你一定遇到过“Unindent does not match any outer indentation level”的错误。 此错误是使用 Python 编程时最常见的错误之一，当您不知道该怎么做时，它可能会令人沮丧。

当您的 Python 代码的缩进不正确时会发生错误。 您可能知道，Python 严重依赖缩进来表示代码块。 缩进代码时很容易出错，从而导致此错误。

为了帮助您解决这个问题，这篇博文将解释错误的原因以及解决方法。

是什么导致错误？

当代码块的缩进与其周围代码的缩进不匹配时，就会发生错误。 换句话说，代码块的缩进级别与代码的外部缩进级别不匹配。

以下是生成错误消息的代码示例：

 def function(): print("This is a block of code") print("This code is not indented correctly.")

在此示例中，第二个 print 语句缩进不正确。 代码块不再与块外的第一个打印语句处于同一缩进级别。 这会导致 Python 抛出错误消息“Unindent does not match any outer indentation level”。

如何修复错误

现在您知道了错误的原因，让我们看看如何修复它。 检查以下部分：

检查代码的缩进

检查代码的缩进级别以查找可能发生不匹配的位置。 通过使用空格或制表符确保缩进大小相同。 决定使用哪个并在整个代码中保持一致。 使用带有缩进指南的代码编辑器

避免缩进错误的更好方法是使用带有缩进指南的代码编辑器。 指南是沿着代码向下显示每行缩进级别的行。 具有缩进指南的代码编辑器的一个很好的例子是 Visual Studio Code。

小心使用“Tab”键

虽然可以在 Python 中使用制表符创建缩进，但它可能会产生意外错误。 这是因为制表符和空格在人眼看来可能是一样的，但 Python 解释器可以不同地识别它们。 因此，在使用制表符进行缩进时最好小心。

避免错误的其他提示

  • 始终使用偶数个空格进行缩进
  • 在整个代码中使用一致的缩进
  • 检查混有空格的制表符

结论

当代码块的缩进级别与其周围代码不匹配时，就会发生这种情况。 为避免此错误，请始终检查您的代码是否存在缩进级别的不一致。

您还可以使用带有缩进指南的代码编辑器，在使用“Tab”键时要小心，并在整个代码中使用一致的缩进。 通过遵循这些提示，您可以编写 Python 代码而不会再次遇到此错误。

我们希望这篇博文能够提供信息，帮助您更好地理解“Unindent 不匹配任何外部缩进级别”错误。 编码愉快！



原文:

If you are a Python developer, you must have encountered the “Unindent does not match any outer indentation level” error. This error is one of the most common errors that occur when programming in Python, and it can be frustrating when you don’t know what to do.

The error occurs when the indentation of your Python code is incorrect. As you may know, Python relies heavily on indentation to signify code blocks. It is easy to make mistakes when indenting your code, which leads to this error.

To help you solve this problem, this blog post will explain the cause of the error and how to fix it.

What Causes the Error?

The error occurs when there is a mismatch between the indentation of a block of code and the indentation of its surrounding code. In other words, the code block’s indentation level doesn’t match the outer indentation level of the code.

Here is an example of code that generates the error message:

def function(): print("This is a block of code") print("This code is not indented correctly.")

In this example, the second print statement is indented incorrectly. The code block is no longer at the same indentation level as the first print statement, which is outside the block. This causes Python to throw the error message “Unindent does not match any outer indentation level.”

How to Fix the Error

Now that you know the cause of the error let's look at how to fix it. Check the following sections:

Check the Indentation of Your Code

Check the indentation level of your code to find where the mismatch might be occurring. Ensure that the indentations are all the same size, either by using spaces or tabs. Decide which to use and be consistent throughout your code. Python is strict about spacing.

Use a Code Editor with Indentation Guides

A better way to avoid the indentation error is to use a code editor with indentation guides. The guides are lines that go down your code showing indentation levels per line. A good example of a code editor that has indentation guides is Visual Studio Code.

Use the “Tab” key Carefully

While it is possible to use tabs to create indentations in Python, it could generate unexpected errors. This is because tabs and spaces can look the same to the human eye, but the Python interpreter can recognize them differently. Therefore, it’s best to be careful when using tabs for indentation.

Additional Tips to Avoid the Error

  • Always use an even number of spaces for indentation
  • Use consistent indentation throughout the code
  • Check for tabs mixed with spaces

Conclusion

The “Unindent does not match any outer indentation level” error is one of the most common errors in Python. It happens when there’s a mismatch between the indentation level of a code block and its surrounding code. To avoid this error, always check your code for inconsistencies in indentation levels.

You can also use a code editor with indentation guides, be careful when using the "Tab" key, and use consistent indentation throughout your code. By following these tips, you can write Python code without encountering this error again.

We hope this blog post was informative and helped you understand the “Unindent does not match any outer indentation level” error better. Happy coding!

item.title: AWS CDK ARM64 CodeBuild 损坏 | AWS CDK ARM64 CodeBuild is broken
item.description:
托马斯·苏斯特

发表于 4 月 5 日

很多噪音，但一如既往的现实表明什么是真实的。

下面是一个应该工作的 CDK 管道的简单示例

pipelines.CodePipeline( self, "pipeline", self_mutation_code_build_defaults=pipelines.CodeBuildOptions( partial_build_spec=codebuild.BuildSpec.from_object( { "phases": { "install": { "commands": [ "npm cache clean -f", "n 16", "node -v", ] } } } ) ), code_build_defaults=pipelines.CodeBuildOptions( role_policy=[ iam.PolicyStatement(actions=["*"], resources=["*"]), iam.PolicyStatement(actions=["sts:AssumeRole"], resources=["*"]), ], build_environment=codebuild.BuildEnvironment( compute_type=codebuild.ComputeType.LARGE, build_image=codebuild.LinuxBuildImage.AMAZON_LINUX_2_ARM_2, ), ), synth=pipelines.ShellStep( "synth", input=pipelines.CodePipelineSource.connection( repo_string=pipeline_vars.github_git_repo, branch="main", connection_arn=pipeline_vars.github_connection_arn, ), install_commands=[ "npm cache clean -f", "n 16", "node -v", "pip install -r requirements.txt", "npm install -g aws-cdk", ], commands=[ "cdk synth", ], ), pipeline_name=pipeline_vars.project, self_mutation=True, )

我们需要记住的是，AWS 不提供包含受支持节点版本的更新构建映像，最好是 16 或 18，但它们坚持使用节点 12！

上面的管道强制安装节点 16 以进行管道合成和自突变。

一旦暂停，airmonitor 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

一旦解除暂停，airmonitor 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，airmonitor 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 airmonitor 没有暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 airmonitor 的操作：

降低 airmonitor 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 airmonitor 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Tomasz Szuster

Posted on Apr 5

That's so sad that AWS is not doing a good job with undifferentiated heavy lifting.
A lot of noise but the reality as usual shows what is true.

Below is a simple example of CDK Pipelines which should work

pipelines.CodePipeline(
            self,
            "pipeline",
            self_mutation_code_build_defaults=pipelines.CodeBuildOptions(
                partial_build_spec=codebuild.BuildSpec.from_object(
                    {
                        "phases": {
                            "install": {
                                "commands": [
                                    "npm cache clean -f",
                                    "n 16",
                                    "node -v",
                                ]
                            }
                        }
                    }
                )
            ),
            code_build_defaults=pipelines.CodeBuildOptions(
                role_policy=[
                    iam.PolicyStatement(actions=["*"], resources=["*"]),
                    iam.PolicyStatement(actions=["sts:AssumeRole"], resources=["*"]),
                ],
                build_environment=codebuild.BuildEnvironment(
                    compute_type=codebuild.ComputeType.LARGE,
                    build_image=codebuild.LinuxBuildImage.AMAZON_LINUX_2_ARM_2,
                ),
            ),
            synth=pipelines.ShellStep(
                "synth",
                input=pipelines.CodePipelineSource.connection(
                    repo_string=pipeline_vars.github_git_repo,
                    branch="main",
                    connection_arn=pipeline_vars.github_connection_arn,
                ),
                install_commands=[
                    "npm cache clean -f",
                    "n 16",
                    "node -v",
                    "pip install -r requirements.txt",
                    "npm install -g aws-cdk",
                ],
                commands=[
                    "cdk synth",
                ],
            ),
            pipeline_name=pipeline_vars.project,
            self_mutation=True,
        )

What we need to remember is that AWS doesn't provide updated build image that contain supported node version, ideally 16 or 18, but they stick with node 12!

The pipeline above is enforcing node 16 installation for pipeline synth and self-mutation.

Once suspended, airmonitor will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, airmonitor will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by airmonitor will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If airmonitor is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag airmonitor:

Make all posts by airmonitor less visible

airmonitor consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging airmonitor will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 为初学者分解 Defi 术语。 | Breaking down Defi terms for Beginners.
item.description:
弗兰克·奥佐罗

发表于 4 月 5 日

让我们从定义什么是Defi开始

去中心化交易所（Dex）

这是一个点对点市场，交易直接发生在加密货币交易者之间，无需中介来促进资金的转移和保管。 去中心化交易所依靠智能合约允许交易者在没有中介的情况下执行订单。 另一方面，中心化交易所由中心化组织管理，例如银行，该组织以其他方式参与金融服务以谋取利润。 最受欢迎的去中心化交易所是 Uniswap，日均交易量超过 40 亿美元。

自动机械制造商

自动做市商 (AMM) 是一种允许在去中心化交易所 (DEX) 上以市场价格自动买卖数字资产的系统，无需中介。 在 AMM 中，任何人都可以成为流动性提供者。 这些流动性提供者需要在交易对中存入等值的每项资产，然后通过持有的加密货币赚取交易费来激励他们。 如果他们试图存入比另一种资产更多的一种资产，则池背后的智能合约会使交易无效。

虽然与订单簿交易所相比，它们确实有其局限性，但它们为加密货币带来的整体创新是无价的。

流动性

加密货币的流动性仅仅意味着一种数字货币或代币可以在不影响价格的情况下轻松转换为另一种数字资产或现金，反之亦然。 简单来说，流动性描述了一种资产可以多快多容易地被买卖。 从这个意义上说，具有良好流动性的资产意味着可以快速轻松地买卖该资产，而不会对其价格产生太大影响。

流动资金池

流动资金池是当前 DeFi 生态系统背后的基础技术之一。 它们是自动做市商 (AMM) 的重要组成部分。 流动性池是在 DeFi 平台上锁定在智能合约中的加密资产的集合，以确保资产在去中心化交易中的流动性。 它们有助于确保充足的流动性，以随时促进去中心化交易。 当用户将他们的加密货币锁定到智能合约中并允许其他人使用时，就会创建流动资金池。

流动性提供者

在流动性池中提供流动性的人称为“流动性提供者”。 作为提供流动性的交换，他们从池中发生的交易中赚取交易费。 这些费用根据流动性提供者向池中贡献的资金量按比例分配给流动性提供者。 流动性提供者通过 LP 代币赚取费用，LP 代币代表他们在池中贡献的资本份额。

商人

交易者只是想要在去中心化交易所买卖加密资产的个人。 交易者不同于流动性提供者。 交易者利用流动性提供者提供的流动性在去中心化交易所中交易/交换加密货币，而流动性提供者为这些代币提供流动性并赚取交易费作为激励。

滑移

滑点是去中心化交易所中非常重要的问题。 滑点只是指您在启动交易时在屏幕上看到的价格与执行隔夜利息的实际价格之间的差异。 出现这种差异是因为在您在区块链上发送交易和交易被挖掘之间存在短暂（有时很长，取决于网络拥塞）延迟。 由于加密货币的波动性，在这些等待时间内，随着其他交易的发生，最终执行交易的价格环境将会发生变化。 在交易未决期间，价格影响和滑点都可能发生变化，许多去中心化交易所（如 Uniswap）将安全检查纳入其协议，以保护最终用户免受掉期/交易执行环境的剧烈变化。

设置高滑点容忍度将使交易者容易受到三明治或抢先交易攻击，这超出了本主题的范围

费用

当交易者在去中心化交易所进行掉期交易时，他们将被收取 0.01% –1% 的交易费。 这些费用然后根据他们在池中的份额分配给流动性提供者，作为在池中提供流动性的激励。 基点通常用于计算 Defi 和各种金融市场的费用百分比，但这超出了本主题的范围。

套利

套利是 Defi 中的一种常见交易做法，它利用跨多个交易所的一种资产的价格差异。 例如，交易者可以在一个市场购买特定资产，然后在另一个市场以更高的价格迅速出售相同的资产。

永久损失

向流动性池提供流动性可能是一项有利可图的冒险，但我们需要牢记无常损失的概念。 当您向流动性池提供流动性时，就会发生无常损失，并且您存入资产的价格与存入时相比会发生变化。 这种变化越大，你就越容易遭受无常损失。 在这种情况下，损失意味着取款时的美元价值低于存款时的美元价值。 池中资产的波动性越大，您就越有可能遭受无常损失。 池中两种货币中的哪一种货币价格上涨或下跌并不重要。 结果将是无常的损失。

之所以称为无常损失，是因为损失只有在您从流动性池中提取资产后才会实现。 然而，到那时，损失就变成了永久性的。 您赚取的费用可能能够弥补这些损失。

总锁定价值 (TVL) 是评估 DeFi 协议的重要指标。 许多交易者和投资者使用这种简单的衡量标准来了解协议的实力和潜力。 它已成为评估 DeFi 项目的关键指标，因为它提供了衡量协议总体健康状况的有用指标。 现在就这些，希望你觉得这有帮助。 您可以在评论部分添加更多内容。

一旦暂停，frankozoalor 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

解除暂停后，frankozoalor 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，frankozoalor 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 frankozoalor 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 frankozoalor：

降低 frankozoalor 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

不屈不挠的 frankozoalor 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Frank Ozoalor

Posted on Apr 5

Let’s start by defining what Defi is

DeFi is a segment of blockchain technology that comprises financial products and services that are accessible to anyone with an internet connection and operates without the involvement of banks or any other third-party firms.

Decentralized Exchange (Dex)

This is a peer-to-peer marketplace where transactions occur directly between crypto traders without the need for an intermediary to facilitate the transfer and custody of funds. Decentralized exchanges rely on smart contracts to allow traders to execute orders without an intermediary. On the other hand, centralized exchanges are managed by a centralized organization such as a bank that is otherwise involved in financial services looking to make a profit. The most popular decentralized exchange is Uniswap with a daily average trading volume of over $4 billion dollars.

AMM

An automated market maker (AMM) is a system that allows digital assets to be bought and sold automatically at market price on a decentralized exchange (DEX), without an intermediary. This can happen with the concept of liquidity pool and liquidity providers on an AMM. In an AMM, anyone can become a liquidity provider. These liquidity providers need to deposit an equivalent value of each asset in the trading pair and in turn are incentivized by earning trading fees on their cryptocurrency holdings. If they attempt to deposit more of one asset than the other, the smart contract behind the pool invalidates the transaction.

AMM enables essentially anyone to create markets seamlessly and efficiently. While they do have their limitations compared to order book exchanges, the overall innovation they bring to crypto is invaluable.

 Liquidity

Liquidity in cryptocurrency simply means the ease with which a digital currency or token can be converted to another digital asset or cash without impacting the price and vice-versa. In simple terms, liquidity describes how quickly and easily an asset can be bought or sold. In this sense, an assets with good liquidity means that the asset can be quickly and easily bought or sold without having much effect on its price.

 Liquidity pool

Liquidity pools are one of the foundational technologies behind the current DeFi ecosystem. They are an essential part of automated market makers (AMM). A liquidity pool is a collection of crypto assets locked in a smart contract on a DeFi platform to ensure liquidity of assets in decentralized trading. They help to ensure sufficient liquidity to facilitate decentralized trade at any time. Liquidity pools are created when users lock their cryptocurrency into smart contracts and enable them to be used by others.

Liquidity provider 

A Person who provides liquidity in a liquidity pool is called a ‘liquidity provider’. In exchange for providing their liquidity, they earn trading fees from the trades that happen in their pool. These fees are distributed proportionally to liquidity providers based on the amount of capital they contributed to the pool. Liquidity providers earn fees through LP tokens, which are tokens that represent their share of capital contributed to the pool.

 Trader 

A trader is simply an individual who wants to buy or sell crypto assets in a decentralized exchange. Traders are different from Liquidity providers. A trader makes use of the liquidity provided by Liquidity provider to trade/swap cryptocurrencies in a decentralized exchange, while a liquidity provider provides liquidity for these tokens and earn trading fees as incentive.

Slippage 

Slippage is a very important issue in decentralized exchanges. Slippage simply means the difference between the price that you see on the screen when initialing a transaction and the actual price the swap is executed at. This difference appears because there’s a short (and sometimes long, depending on network congestion) delay between when you send a transaction on the blockchain and when it gets mined. Due to the volatility of cryptocurrency, during these waiting time, the price environment in which the transaction will eventually be executed will change, as other trades will be taking place. Price impact and slippage can both change while a transaction is pending, alot of decentralized exchanges like Uniswap put safety checks into their protocol to protect end-users from drastic changes in the execution environment of their swap/transaction.

Setting a high slippage tolerance will leave a trader open to sandwich or front-running attack which is beyond the scope of this topic

 Fees 

When a trader makes a swap on a decentralized exchange, They are charged a trading fee ranging from 0.01% –1%. These fees are then distributed to liquidity providers according to their share in the pool as an incentive for providing liquidity in the pool. Basis points are commonly used to calculate fee percentages in Defi and across various financial markets but this is beyond the scope of this thread. 

 Arbitrage 

Arbitrage is a common trading practice in Defi that exploits price discrepancies in one asset across multiple exchanges. For instance, a trader could buy a particular asset in one market and quickly sell the same asset in another market, at a higher price.

 Impermanent Loss

Providing liquidity to a liquidity pool can be a profitable venture, but we’ll need to keep the concept of impermanent loss in mind.  Impermanent loss happens when you provide liquidity to a liquidity pool, and the price of your deposited assets changes compared to when you deposited them. The bigger this change is, the more you are exposed to impermanent loss. In this case, the loss means less dollar value at the time of withdrawal than at the time of deposit. The more volatile the assets are in the pool, the more likely it is that you can be exposed to impermanent loss. It doesn’t matter which of the two currencies in the pool undergoes an increase or decrease in price. The result will be an impermanent loss.

It’s called impermanent loss because the losses only become realized once you withdraw your assets from the liquidity pool. At that point, however, the losses very much become permanent. The fees you earn may be able to compensate for those losses. 

TVL

Total locked value (TVL) is an important metric for assessing DeFi protocols. Many of traders and investors use this simple measure to understand a protocol’s strength and potential. TVL is the value of all the assets contained within a DeFi protocol. It has become a key metric for appraising DeFi projects because it provides a useful measure of a protocol’s general health. DefiLiama is a good platform to check the TVL in various Defi protocol. 

Thats it for now, Hope you find this helpful. you can add more in the comment section.

item.title: Apache AGE 入门：综合指南（第 1 部分） | Getting Started with Apache AGE: A Comprehensive Guide (Part 1)
item.description:
蒂托·奥萨德贝

发表于 4 月 5 日

介绍

在本指南中，我们将介绍开始使用 Apache AGE 所需了解的所有内容。 无论您是经验丰富的图形数据库用户还是刚刚起步，本指南都将为您提供使用 Apache AGE 的详细过程。 在第一部分中，我们将介绍 Apache AGE 的介绍和安装。 到本教程结束时，您应该对 Apache AGE 有深入的了解并安装了它。

什么是 Apache AGE？

它是作为 PostgreSQL 的扩展而构建的，支持复杂的图形查询并提供快速的查询响应时间，使其成为传统关系数据库管理系统的绝佳选择。 借助 Apache AGE，用户可以更深入地了解数据并做出更明智的决策。 设置和安装

因此，您应该在您的设备上安装 PostgreSQL 11 或 12（截至本文发布时，AGE 仅与这些版本兼容）。
有关安装所需版本的分步指南，请单击此处。
安装 PostgreSQL 后，按照以下步骤安装 AGE：

安装后，我们初始化一个数据库集群，在 PostgreSQL 上设置 AGE。

接下来，我们启动一个服务器。

 pg_ctl -D age_test -l logfile start createdb age_testdb

启动 PostgreSQL，创建扩展，加载扩展并设置search_path 。

 psql age_testdb CREATE EXTENSION age; LOAD 'age'; SET search_path = ag_catalog, “$user”, public;

初始化数据库后，必须加载 AGE 扩展才能开始使用它。 这可以在位于 DB 目录的postgresql.conf中设置，这将使 AGE 自动加载并设置search_path 。 查看已安装的扩展：

如果列出了 age，那么它已经成功安装。

结论

设置 Apache AGE 只是释放图形数据分析功能的第一步。 使用 Apache AGE，我们可以创建复杂的图形，轻松存储、管理和查询大规模图形数据。 随着我们在本指南中的推进，我们将探索 Apache AGE 的高级特性以及如何有效地使用这个强大的系统。 在下一部分中，我们将着眼于创建和分析图表。

参考
阿帕奇年龄网站
戴夫帕森斯 - 22 年 12 月 31 日

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 titoausten 的方法：

使 titoausten 的所有帖子不那么显眼

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 titoausten 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Tito Osadebey

Posted on Apr 5

   

Introduction

In this guide, we will walk through everything you need to know to get started with Apache AGE. Whether you’re an experienced graph database user or you’re just starting out, this guide will provide you with a detailed process to working with Apache AGE. In this first part, we’ll cover the introduction and installation of Apache AGE. By the end of this tutorial, you should have a deep understanding of Apache AGE and have it installed.

What is Apache AGE?

Apache AGE is an open-source graph database management system that provides a powerful and scalable solution for storing, managing and analyzing large-scale graph data. It is built as an extension of PostgreSQL and supports complex graph queries and provides fast query response times, making it an excellent choice over traditional relational database management systems. With Apache AGE, users can gain deeper insights into data and make more informed decisions. Apache AGE is a tool you won’t want to overlook as a developer, data scientist or graph expert.

Setting up and Installation

Apache AGE is built as an extension to PostgreSQl. Hence, you should have either PostgreSQL 11 or 12 installed on your device (AGE is only compatible with these versions as of the time of this post).
For a step-by-step guide to install the required version, click here.
With PostgreSQL installed, follow the steps below to install AGE:

1. Clone the AGE repository in the same directory where you installed PostgreSQL.

git clone https://github.com/apache/age.git

2. Move into the repository and change the branch to the latest version which is v1.1.0.

cd age
git checkout release/PG12/1.1.0

3. Make PG_CONFIG an environment variable and install.

export PG_CONFIG=/usr/local/pgsql-12/bin/pg_config 
sudo make install
make installcheck

After installation, we initialize a database cluster to set up AGE on PostgreSQL.

Next, we start a server.

pg_ctl -D age_test -l logfile start
createdb age_testdb

Start up PostgreSQL, create the extension, load the extension and set the search_path.

psql age_testdb
CREATE EXTENSION age;
LOAD ‘age’;
SET search_path = ag_catalog, “$user”, public;

After initializing a DB, the AGE extension has to be loaded to start using it. This can be set in the postgresql.conf located in the DB directory which will make AGE load automatically and also set the search_path. To view installed extensions:

If age is listed, then it has been successfully installed.

Conclusion

Setting up Apache AGE is just the first step in unlocking the power of graph data analytics. With Apache AGE, we can create complex graphs, store, manage, and query large-scale graph data with ease. As we move forward in this guide, we will explore the advanced features of Apache AGE and how to use this powerful system efficiently. In the next part, we would look at creating and analyzing graphs.

References
Apache AGE website
Apache AGE project

Dave Parsons - Dec 31 '22

item.title: 函数声明与函数表达式 | function declaration vs function expression
item.description:
在 JavaScript 中，有两种常见的函数定义方式：函数声明和函数表达式。

函数声明是声明具有指定参数集的命名函数的语句。 这是一个例子：

 function addNumbers(a, b) { return a + b; }

另一方面，函数表达式是一个赋值语句，其中将函数分配给变量或属性。 这是一个例子：

 const addNumbers = function(a, b) { return a + b; };

函数声明和函数表达式之间的主要区别是函数声明被提升到它们作用域的顶部，而函数表达式不是。

这意味着您可以在代码中定义之前调用使用函数声明声明的函数，但不能对函数表达式执行相同的操作。 例如：

 console.log(addNumbers(2, 3)); // Output: 5 function addNumbers(a, b) { return a + b; }

在这种情况下，函数 addNumbers 被提升到代码的顶部，因此可以在定义之前调用它。

但是，如果您尝试对函数表达式执行相同操作，则会出现错误：

 console.log(addNumbers(2, 3)); // Output: Uncaught ReferenceError: addNumbers is not defined const addNumbers = function(a, b) { return a + b; };

通常，对需要在定义之前调用的函数使用函数声明，对将分配给变量或属性的函数使用函数表达式是一种很好的做法。

一旦暂停，himanshudevgupta 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，himanshudevgupta 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，himanshudevgupta 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 himanshudevgupta 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 himanshudevgupta 的方法：

降低 himanshudevgupta 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 himanshudevgupta 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

In JavaScript, there are two common ways to define a function: function declaration and function expression.

A function declaration is a statement that declares a named function with a specified set of parameters. Here's an example:

function addNumbers(a, b) {
  return a + b;
}

A function expression, on the other hand, is an assignment statement where the function is assigned to a variable or property. Here's an example:

const addNumbers = function(a, b) {
  return a + b;
};

The main difference between function declaration and function expression is that function declarations are hoisted to the top of their scope, whereas function expressions are not.

This means that you can call a function declared with a function declaration before it is defined in the code, but you cannot do the same with a function expression. For example:

console.log(addNumbers(2, 3)); // Output: 5

function addNumbers(a, b) {
  return a + b;
}

In this case, the function addNumbers is hoisted to the top of the code, so it can be called before it is defined.

However, if you try to do the same with a function expression, you will get an error:

console.log(addNumbers(2, 3)); // Output: Uncaught ReferenceError: addNumbers is not defined

const addNumbers = function(a, b) {
  return a + b;
};

In general, it's a good practice to use function declarations for functions that need to be called before they are defined, and function expressions for functions that will be assigned to variables or properties.

→ But function expression can only invoke/run/call "after" the function definition.

→ If we call the function expression before initialization, then this will give an error.

→Function declarations are hoisted but function expressions are not.

Archimedes Fagundes Junior - Apr 3

Arshad Ali Soomro - Apr 3

Jakub Andrzejewski - Apr 3

item.title: 使用 Next.js 国际化和 next-i18next 翻译网站内容 | Translate website content using Next.js internationalization and next-i18next
item.description:
深入了解 Nextjs 的国际化路由

您打算为您的网站添加多语言支持吗？

在本文中，我们将指导您完成使用 Next.js 12 国际化和 next-i18next 翻译网站内容的步骤。

什么是 Next.js 国际化？

您网站的多种语言版本的路由和呈现由 Next.js 自动处理。 可以使用 Next.js 确定用户的首选语言，然后它可以提供适当的内容。

为什么是 next-i18next？

您可以通过在 Next.js 项目中应用 next-i18next 包，快速将国际化 (i18n) 添加到您的网站。 这意味着您可以将您网站上的信息翻译成多种语言，以便更广泛的受众访问。

文件夹结构

├── public │ ├── locales │ │ ├── en │ │ │ ├── common.json │ │ │ └── blog.json │ │ └── fr │ │ ├── common.json │ │ └── blog.json ├── pages │ ├── _app.tsx │ └── index.tsx ├── next.config.js └── next-i18next.config.js

开始

在终端中导航到您的项目目录，运行以下命令：

npm install next-i18next

添加翻译支持

您需要创建一个翻译文件来为您的 Next.js 应用程序添加翻译支持。 这些文件将包含不同语言的翻译字符串（ next-i18next uses json ）
创建一个名为public/locales/的新文件夹，并为每个语言环境创建一个单独的文件夹。 例如，分别为English和French翻译创建en/blog.json和fr/blog.json 。

 public/locales ├── en │ ├── common.json │ └── blog.json └── fr ├── common.json └── blog.json

以下是翻译文件的示例：

 //locale/en/blog.json { "welcome": "Welcome to my blog!" }
 //locale/fr/blog.json { "welcome": "Bienvenue sur mon blog!" }

上面的例子都使用“welcome”作为键

配置next-i18next

接下来，配置 next-i18next 以使用翻译文件。 在项目的根目录下创建一个next-i18next.config.js文件并添加以下配置：

 // next-i18next.config.js module.exports = { i18n: { defaultLocale: "en", //default locale locales: ["en", "fr"] // list of supported locales }, };

现在在项目根目录下的next.config.js文件中导入i18n

 /** @type {import('next').NextConfig} */ const { i18n } = require("./next-i18next.config"); const nextConfig = { reactStrictMode: true, swcMinify: true, i18n, }; module.exports = nextConfig;

用 appWithTranslation 包装你的 Next.js 应用

现在在_app inside pages 文件夹中：

 import '../styles/globals.css' import { appWithTranslation } from 'next-i18next' function MyApp({ Component, pageProps }) { return <Component {...pageProps} /> } export default appWithTranslation(MyApp)

这将使用next-i18next提供的必要上下文和翻译功能包装您的 Next.js 应用程序。 这可以在整个应用程序中启用翻译支持。

服务器端翻译

这是一个异步函数，您需要通过 getStaticProps 或 getServerSideProps 将其包含在页面级组件中。 结论

就是这样！ 您现在已经了解了如何使用 Next.js 国际化和 next-i18next 翻译网站内容。 使用 Next.js 和 next-i18next，创建多语言网站从未如此简单。

编码愉快！



原文:

Dive into Nextjs's internationalized routing

Are you planning to add multi-language support for your website?

In this article, we'll guide you through the steps to translate website content using Next.js 12 internationalization and next-i18next.

What is Next.js Internationalization?

A built-in feature of Next.js Internationalization enables you to develop a website with content in various languages. The routing and rendering of several language versions of your website are handled automatically by Next.js. The user's preferred language can be determined using Next.js, which can then offer the appropriate content.

Why next-i18next?

You may quickly add internationalization (i18n) to your website by applying the next-i18next package in a Next.js project. This means that you can offer translations of the information on your website into several languages, making it accessible to a broader audience.

Folder Structure 

├── public
│   ├── locales
│   │   ├── en
│   │   │   ├── common.json
│   │   │   └── blog.json
│   │   └── fr
│   │       ├── common.json
│   │       └── blog.json
├── pages
│   ├── _app.tsx
│   └── index.tsx
├── next.config.js
└── next-i18next.config.js

Get Started

Navigate to your project directory in the terminal run the following command:

npm install next-i18next

Adding Translation Support

You need to create a translation file to add translation support to your Next.js application. These files will contain the translated strings for different languages (next-i18next uses json)
Create a new folder named public/locales/ and create a separate folder for each locale. For example, create en/blog.json, and fr/blog.json for English, and French translations, respectively.

public/locales
├── en
│   ├── common.json
│   └── blog.json
└── fr
    ├── common.json
    └── blog.json

common.json is the default json file that next-i18next will look into if no existing translation file.

Here's an example of what a translation file might look like:

//locale/en/blog.json
{ "welcome": "Welcome to my blog!"
}

//locale/fr/blog.json
{ "welcome": "Bienvenue sur mon blog!"
}

⚠️The keys must match both json files for the translation to work. The example above both use "welcome" as key

Configure next-i18next

Next, configure next-i18next to work with the translation files. Create a next-i18next.config.js file in the root of your project and add the following configuration:

// next-i18next.config.js
module.exports = { i18n: { defaultLocale: "en", //default locale locales: ["en", "fr"] // list of supported locales },
};

Now import i18n in next.config.js file in the root of your project

/** @type {import('next').NextConfig} */
const { i18n } = require("./next-i18next.config");
const nextConfig = { reactStrictMode: true, swcMinify: true, i18n,
}; module.exports = nextConfig;

Wrap Your Next.js App with appWithTranslation

Now in _app inside pages folder:

import '../styles/globals.css'
import { appWithTranslation } from 'next-i18next' function MyApp({ Component, pageProps }) { return <Component {...pageProps} />
} export default appWithTranslation(MyApp)

This wraps your Next.js application with the necessary context and translation functionality provided by next-i18next. This enables translation support throughout your application.

ServerSideTranslations

This is an async function that you need to include on your page-level components via either getStaticProps or getServerSideProps. (depending on the use case)

//pages/index
import { serverSideTranslations } from "next-i18next/serverSideTranslations";
import { useTranslation } from 'next-i18next' function Home() { const { t } = useTranslation('blog') return ( <div> <h1>{t('welcome')}</h1> </div> )
} //getServeSideProps wors too
export const getStaticProps = async ({ locale }) => { return { props: { ...(await serverSideTranslations(locale, ["blog"])), }, };
};

Conclusion

That's it! You've now learned how to translate website content using Next.js internationalization and next-i18next. With Next.js and next-i18next, creating a multilingual website has never been easier.

Happy coding!
